channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Congress has the power to halt Trump's tariffs. But Republicans aren't ready to use it
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As stock markets tumble in the aftermath of President Donald Trump's sweeping tariffs, Republicans in Congress were watching with unease and talking of clawing back their power to levy tariffs — but almost none seemed ready to turn their words into action.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man suspected of strangling pregnant teen girlfriend to death release on bond for the second time
item.description: A Georgia man accused of killing his pregnant teenage girlfriend in August of 2024 was granted bond a second time after being hit with more charges, including statutory rape.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump to give TikTok another 75-day extension
item.description: President Trump said Friday that he plans to give TikTok another 75-day extension as his administration nears a deal to avert a ban on the popular social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Sean 'Diddy' Combs hit with new sex trafficking charges a month before trial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal prosecutors on Friday added two charges to Sean “Diddy” Combs ' indictment and said they expect four accusers to testify against him, expanding on allegations that the jailed hip-hop mogul engaged in sex trafficking with multiple women and as recently as last year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Rachel Morin’s alleged illegal killer left slain jogger’s body along 150-foot blood trail: prosecutors
item.description: Opening statements began in the trial of an illegal immigrant charged with murdering Rachel Morin, a Maryland mother of five who was killed while jogging on a hiking trail.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump calls for Marine Le Pen to be freed
item.description: President Trump defended embattled French far-right leader Marine Le Pen, arguing there's a “witch hunt” against her.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Longtime Washington Post columnist Eugene Robinson is leaving. He cited Bezos' new editorial policy
item.description: Washington Post columnist Eugene Robinson is leaving the newspaper, the second such veteran to exit in the wake of owner Jeff Bezos' directive that the Post narrow the topics covered by its opinion section to personal liberties and the free market.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Microsoft's AI division head wants to create a lasting relationship between chatbots and their users
item.description: Fifty years after the founding of Microsoft, the CEO of its artificial intelligence division has a big task: develop a new product line as integral to daily life as the software giant’s past innovations.
item.dc:creator: Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: British comedian charged with rape and sexual assault
item.description: LONDON (AP) — British police on Friday charged Russell Brand with rape and sexual assault following an 18-month investigation sparked when four women alleged they had been assaulted by the controversial comedian.
item.dc:creator: JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Viral video shows Florida charter captain arguing with boater; police investigating
item.description: A viral video showing a fight between two Florida boaters has sparked both a police investigation and strong reactions from people online.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal officials are quietly terminating the legal residency of some international college students
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A crackdown on foreign students is alarming college leaders, who say the Trump administration is using new tactics and vague justifications to push some students out of the country. College officials worry the new approach will keep foreigners from wanting to study in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, ANNIE MA and MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Comedian Russell Brand charged with rape and sexual assault in UK
item.description: British police on Friday charged Russell Brand with rape and sexual assault following an 18-month investigation sparked when four women alleged they had been assaulted by the controversial comedian.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Karen Read appeals double jeopardy ruling to US Supreme Court
item.description: Karen Read's lawyers have taken her double jeopardy appeal to the United States Supreme Court with her second trial underway in the death of John O'Keefe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bondi announces charges in MS-13 killing as the Trump administration highlights its gang crackdown
item.description: FORT LAUDERDALE, Fla. (AP) — Three alleged MS-13 gang members have been federally charged in connection with a killing a decade ago in Florida, U.S. Attorney General Pam Bondi announced Friday, seeking to highlight the Trump administration's push to prosecute violent gangs.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CEO allegedly choked fellow cruise ship passenger over dance floor dispute: FBI
item.description: First American Financial CEO Kenneth DeGiorgio is facing federal charges after allegedly choking another passenger while on a Virgin Voyages cruise, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How Trump's latest tariffs could affect your wallet
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President Donald Trump has unveiled his latest tariffs, and they could have significant implications for your wallet. Trump's sweeping new tariffs, on top of previous levies and retaliation worldwide, are expected to increase prices for everyday items.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS and CORA LEWIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: What to know about the severe storms and flash flooding hitting parts of the US
item.description: LITTLE ROCK, Ark. (AP) — At least seven people have been killed in a wide swath of violent storms and tornadoes that hit the South and Midwest, and officials are bracing for more severe weather and flooding in the coming days.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump ordered to pay legal fees in ‘Steele Dossier’ lawsuit
item.description: A judge in the United Kingdom has ordered President Donald Trump to pay more than $820,000 in legal fees to the company representing former British spy Christopher Steele after he unsuccessfully sued over a dossier.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration issues demands on Harvard as conditions for billions in federal money
item.description: Some alumni want Harvard to resist Trump administration demands the university must meet as a condition for receiving $9 billion in federal grants and contracts being threatened during an investigation into campus antisemitism.
item.dc:creator: Collin Binkley, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Invited to the Met Gala, nothing to wear? Hint: Find yourself a 'superfine' suit
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — What’s in a suit? According to curators busy prepping the newest Met Gala exhibit, a whole lot more than tailoring: history, culture, identity, power and, most of all, self-expression.
item.dc:creator: JOCELYN NOVECK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Yale fires legal scholar amid review of possible ties to 'sham charity' for designated terror group
item.description: Yale Law School has fired an Iranian scholar, accusing her of refusing to cooperate as it probed allegations that she is involved with a group that the U.S. calls a “sham charity” for a designated terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Morgan Wallen selling new merch inspired by remark after abrupt 'SNL' departure
item.description: Morgan Wallen's website is selling t-shirts with a phrase connected to his controversial exit from "Saturday Night Live.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Yankees OF Brett Gardner's son died of carbon monoxide poisoning, had 'layer' on organs, officials say
item.description: Randall Zúñiga, director of the Judicial Investigation Agency, said they tested the 14-year-old Miller Gardner for carboxyhemoglobin, a compound generated when carbon monoxide binds to hemoglobin in the blood.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Girl, 7, struck, killed by falling boulder at Lake Tahoe ski resort
item.description: A 7-year-old girl was killed by a falling boulder at a popular ski resort in Lake Tahoe over the weekend, according to multiple media reports.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KRON4

item.title: A historic Black church took the Proud Boys to court. Now it controls their trademark
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There is so much history between the walls of Metropolitan African Methodist Episcopal Church, which has hosted funerals for Rosa Parks and Frederick Douglass and opened its pews to American presidents and civil rights icons.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Markets plunge in reaction to Trump 'Liberation Day' tariffs
item.description: Stocks plunged Thursday morning as U.S. trading opened for the first time after President Trump’s announcement of heavy tariffs on nearly every nation exporting products to the United States.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump gambles on tariffs as nation braces for economic fallout
item.description: President Trump took his biggest gamble yet on the economy Wednesday when he pressed forward with sweeping tariffs on imports from nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: 1 person dead as violent storms, including tornadoes, wallop Midwest and South
item.description: Tornadoes and violent storms struck parts of the South and Midwest on Wednesday, killing at least one person, knocking down power lines and trees, ripping roofs off homes and shooting debris thousands of feet into the air.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court sides with the FDA in its dispute over sweet-flavored vaping products
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court on Wednesday ruled for the Food and Drug Administration in its crackdown on sweet-flavored vaping products following a surge in teen electronic cigarette use.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Diddy civil suit thrown out after accuser refuses to identify herself
item.description: An accuser's lawsuit against Sean "Diddy" Combs was thrown out this week after she refused to disclose her identity.
item.dc:creator: Diana Falzone, Fox 5

item.title: HHS fires entire staff of program that helps low-income people afford heat and air conditioning
item.description: The HHS fired the entire staff of a program that helps low-income people afford heat and air conditioning.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, Fox 5

item.title: Potential Trump tariff plan could cost households thousands: new analysis
item.description: A new analysis from The Budget Lab at Yale shows how much a broad tariff plan could cost U.S. households.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5

item.title: New documents describe physical condition of man allegedly held captive for decades
item.description: An investigator said the man's skin appeared "to be graying," and that his "physical condition resembles one who would be at a point at the end of their life.
item.dc:creator: Jenn Brink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democratic-backed Susan Crawford wins Wisconsin Supreme Court seat, cementing liberal majority
item.description: MADISON, Wis. (AP) — The Democratic-backed candidate for Wisconsin Supreme Court defeated a challenger endorsed by President Donald Trump and billionaire Elon Musk on Tuesday, touting her victory as a win against powerful interests and cementing a liberal majority for at least three more years.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT BAUER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Northern Michigan
item.description: The tip of Michigan's mitten struggled through another day without electricity Tuesday as restless residents tried to stay warm while utilities scrambled to restore power in a region waylaid by weekend freezing rain that brought down countless trees and poles.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration admits it wrongly deported man to prison in El Salvador
item.description: The Trump administration acknowledged that it had mistakenly deported a Salvadoran man protected from removal.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Girl, 9, dies after procedure at California dental office
item.description: A report from the San Diego County Medical Examiner's Office claims the girl was discharged from the facility and remained asleep on the ride home.
item.dc:creator: Delaney White, ABC 4
